title: The Unwilling Umpire
desc: A charity baseball game — and six signed baseballs go missing. ⚾

q.en: What cause was the town baseball game raising money for?
q.zh: 镇上的棒球比赛是为了给什么事业筹款？

options.en: A new library for Green Lawn
options.zh: 给格林朗镇新建一座图书馆

options.en: New baseball equipment for the school according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 给学校买新的棒球装备（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A playground in the town park
options.zh: 镇公园里的一个游乐场

options.en: Camp Challenge, a summer camp for kids with special needs
options.zh: 挑战营——一个为有特殊需要的孩子办的夏令营

explain.en: The kids sold tickets to fill the bleachers, and the money would go to Camp Challenge, a summer camp for children with special needs.
explain.zh: 孩子们卖票把看台坐满，筹到的钱会捐给“挑战营”——一个为有特殊需要的儿童设立的夏令营。

q.en: Who volunteered to be the umpire at the game?
q.zh: 谁自愿担任比赛的裁判（umpire）？

options.en: Pete Unkenholz, a friendly newcomer
options.zh: 皮特·昂肯霍尔茨，一个友善的新来者

options.en: Mr. Thaddeus Pocket, the coach according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 教练撒迪厄斯·波克特先生（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Mr. Dillon, the school principal
options.zh: 校长狄龙先生

options.en: Officer Fallon, the police chief
options.zh: 法伦警官，警察局长

explain.en: Pete Unkenholz was a tall, friendly guy with spiky blond hair who volunteered to umpire. He had even helped set up the bleachers.
explain.zh: 皮特·昂肯霍尔茨是个高个子、友善、留着金色尖发的小伙子，自愿当裁判。他甚至还帮忙搭了看台。

q.en: Why did Pete leave the game and go into the clubhouse?
q.zh: 皮特为什么离开比赛，走进球员休息室？

options.en: To get his allergy medicine (Aller-Green)
options.zh: 去拿他的过敏药（Aller-Green）

options.en: To steal Mr. Pocket's autographed baseballs according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 去偷波克特先生签了名的棒球（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To hide from the crowd
options.zh: 躲开人群

options.en: To talk to Mr. Pocket about the score
options.zh: 去和波克特先生商量比分

explain.en: Pete started sneezing from his allergies and went to the clubhouse for his allergy medicine. The empty Aller-Green box later found in his trash proved his allergies were real.
explain.zh: 皮特因为过敏开始打喷嚏，便去休息室拿过敏药。后来在他垃圾桶里发现的空Aller-Green药盒证明他的过敏是真的。

q.en: What clue at Pete's trailer made the kids think someone else might be involved?
q.zh: 在皮特的拖车房里，什么线索让孩子们怀疑还有别人牵扯其中？

options.en: A torn ticket to the game
options.zh: 一张撕破的比赛门票

options.en: A glove belonging to Mr. Pocket according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 波克特先生的一只手套（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A red-and-blue flannel shirt far too small for Pete
options.zh: 一件红蓝相间的法兰绒衬衫，对皮特来说太小了

options.en: A map of the baseball field
options.zh: 一张棒球场地图

explain.en: Josh noticed a flannel shirt draped on the lawn chair that was much too small to fit big Pete — a hint that someone smaller (his brother Buddy) also stayed there.
explain.zh: 乔什注意到躺椅上搭着一件法兰绒衬衫，对高大的皮特来说太小了——这暗示有个更小的人（他弟弟巴迪）也住那里。

q.en: What did the kids discover about Pete through the Internet and a phone call?
q.zh: 通过上网和打电话，孩子们发现了皮特的什么？

options.en: He used to live in Green Lawn
options.zh: 他以前住在格林朗

options.en: His real first name was Marion and a Maine paper reported 'Marion Unkenholz' stole a bakery van
options.zh: 他的真名是马里恩，缅因州报纸报道“马里恩·昂肯霍尔茨”偷过一辆面包车

options.en: He had once been a police officer
options.zh: 他曾经当过警察

options.en: He was a famous retired baseball player according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他是个有名的退役棒球手（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Searching 'Unkenholz' led them to a Coast Press article about Marion Unkenholz taking a bakery van in Penobscot, Maine. His full name turned out to be Marion Peter Unkenholz.
explain.zh: 搜索“Unkenholz”让他们找到《海岸新闻》一篇关于马里恩·昂肯霍尔茨在缅因州佩诺布斯科特偷面包车的报道。他的全名其实是马里恩·彼得·昂肯霍尔茨。

q.en: Who is Buddy, and why did Pete confess to the theft?
q.zh: 巴迪是谁？皮特为什么承认偷了球？

options.en: Buddy is a friend of Dink; Pete confessed by mistake
options.zh: 巴迪是丁克的朋友；皮特认错是弄错了

options.en: Buddy is Pete's son; Pete confessed to set a good example
options.zh: 巴迪是皮特的儿子；皮特认错是为了做个好榜样

options.en: Buddy is Pete's 14-year-old brother; Pete lied to protect him from blame
options.zh: 巴迪是皮特14岁的弟弟；皮特说谎是为了替他顶罪

options.en: Buddy is the police chief's nephew; Pete was forced to confess according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 巴迪是警察局长的侄子；皮特是被逼认罪的（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Buddy is Pete's younger brother. Pete confessed to stealing the baseballs so that Buddy — whom he wrongly believed had taken them — would not get in trouble. He had done the same thing over the van in Maine.
explain.zh: 巴迪是皮特的弟弟。皮特承认偷球，是为了不让巴迪（他误以为球是被巴迪拿走的）惹上麻烦。在缅因州的面包车事件上他也这样替弟弟顶过罪。

q.en: How did the kids finally track down the real thief?
q.zh: 孩子们最后是怎么找到真正的小偷的？

options.en: They found the Babe Ruth ball for sale online (YuBuy) and emailed the seller for an address
options.zh: 他们在网上(YuBuy)发现那颗有贝比·鲁斯签名的球在卖，于是发邮件向卖家要地址

options.en: They searched Pete's trailer again according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他们又搜了皮特的拖车房（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Buddy confessed to the crime
options.zh: 巴迪认罪了

options.en: Pete finally told them the truth
options.zh: 皮特终于说了实话

explain.en: Mrs. Mackleroy searched YuBuy for 'baseballs signed by Babe Ruth.' They found Mr. Pocket's ball listed for $1,000, emailed the seller, and got back a Green Lawn address.
explain.zh: 麦克勒罗伊太太在YuBuy上搜“有贝比·鲁斯签名的棒球”。他们发现波克特先生的球标价1000美元在卖，于是发邮件给卖家，得到了格林朗的一个地址。

q.en: Who actually stole Mr. Pocket's six autographed baseballs?
q.zh: 究竟是谁偷了波克特先生的六颗签名棒球？

options.en: Pete Unkenholz
options.zh: 皮特·昂肯霍尔茨

options.en: Ted Gundy
options.zh: 泰德·甘迪

options.en: Buddy Unkenholz
options.zh: 巴迪·昂肯霍尔茨

options.en: Fizzo Martin
options.zh: 菲佐·马丁

explain.en: Officer Fallon arrested Ted Gundy after the email reply gave his address (13 Boxwood Lane). The six stolen baseballs were found in his closet.
explain.zh: 邮件回信给出了泰德·甘迪的地址（黄杨巷13号），法伦警官据此逮捕了他。那六颗被盗的棒球在他衣柜里被找到。

q.en: What lesson did Officer Fallon teach Pete about lying?
q.zh: 法伦警官给皮特上了什么关于说谎的课？

options.en: Only children should tell the truth
options.zh: 只有孩子才该说真话

options.en: Lying is always wrong, even to protect someone else
options.zh: 说谎永远是错的，哪怕是为了保护别人

options.en: Lying is smart if it protects your family according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只要保护家人，说谎是聪明的（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Lying is fine as long as no one is hurt
options.zh: 只要没人受伤，说谎没关系

explain.en: Officer Fallon told Pete that lying is wrong, even when done to protect someone else. Pete learned his lesson, and Fallon even began encouraging him to become a policeman.
explain.zh: 法伦警官告诉皮特，说谎是错的，哪怕是为了保护别人。皮特吸取了教训，法伦甚至开始鼓励他将来当警察。

q.en: What was the final score when the game was replayed the next Saturday?
q.zh: 下周六大比赛重打时，最终比分是多少？

options.en: Men 7, Women 6 according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 男人队7，女人队6（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Men 6, Women 3
options.zh: 男人队6，女人队3

options.en: Tie, 6–6
options.zh: 平局，6比6

options.en: Women 7, Men 6
options.zh: 女人队7，男人队6

explain.en: With the bases loaded, Livvy Nugent hit a grand slam in the bottom of the ninth. The women won 7–6, so Josh owed Ruth Rose that ice-cream cone after all.
explain.zh: 满垒时，丽芙·纽金特在第九局下半击出一记满贯全垒打。女人队以7比6获胜，所以乔什到底还是欠了露丝·罗丝那个冰淇淋甜筒。
